title: Add a null/undefined guard to avoid a TypeError
task: `npm test` fails: `displayName` in src/user.js dereferences `user.name` unconditionally, so a null/undefined user throws a TypeError and a missing/empty name leaks through. Add the guards per the spec comment: return the non-empty `name`, otherwise fall back to "Anonymous". Do not modify the tests. Run the tests to verify.
notes: Missing null/undefined guard: the buggy body throws on a null user. The test pin keeps the fallback assertion; file_not_contains rejects the obvious cheat of weakening the happy-path expectation ("Ada") down to the fallback so the buggy code trivially matches.
